name: SocialPilot
description:
SocialPilot simplifies the social media manager’s life, making everything from scheduling to content creation a stress-free affair. With a range of features, it’s no wonder that businesses of all breeds choose SocialPilot to manage their social media presence.

Hootsuite vs. SocialPilot

  1. Customer service and support: SocialPilot’s support team knows their chops, giving it a big leg up over Hootsuite’s inconsistent service
  2. Reporting and analytics: the two platforms have comparable reporting capabilities, meaning former hooters will be right at home using SocialPilot’s analytics dashboard
  3. Reliability: like any platform, SocialPilot encounters occasional bugs, but the team handles them swiftly
  4. Integrations: SocialPilot lacks the breadth of integrations that you might find at Hootsuite, but its integrations and custom solutions let it adapt to users’ specific needs
  5. Platform usability: the tool is incredibly user-friendly, with helpful onboarding documents and a team ready to jump on a call
  6. User interface: the platform is far more intuitive than Hootsuite’s at times confusing dashboard
  7. Pricing: with pricing starting at $30/month, SocialPilot is the budget-friendly pick

Key Features

  • The platform’s bulk scheduling is amazing, letting you schedule up to 500 posts in advance using a simple CSV file (with images, too).
  • A content calendar helps you visualize your strategy, making it easy to plan and organize your roadmap for world domination.
  • The analytics dashboard is great, too, providing detailed analytics and customizable reports.
  • Collaboration tools allow teams to assign roles and manage workflows within the platform.
  • Client management capabilities let teams manage multiple clients without sharing login credentials, making it easier for agencies to handle client accounts.
  • A social inbox lets you manage comments and DMs in one place.
  • Content curation capabilities are key for discovering relevant content and coming up with post ideas.
  • A handy dandy browser extension means that users can easily schedule and share content while browsing the web on Chrome, Firefox, or Safari.
  • Available on iOS and Android, the SocialPilot mobile app lets users manage their socials on the go.

SocialPilot stands out for its affordability, making it the best value-for-money choice. And with unique features like bulk scheduling, recurring posts, and more, the choice comes down to what your specific business prioritizes. 

‍
